first_diff.message: expected=...s" data-diagram-type="ACTIVITY" height="301px" preserveAspectRatio="none" style=... actual=...s" data-diagram-type="ACTIVITY" height="245px" preserveAspectRatio="none" style=...

underlying_signals.label: Shared richtext table/display
underlying_signals.reason: Source contains Creole/table markup but Rust emits far fewer text or line elements than Java/reference, which points to Display/Creole table semantics rather than Graphviz coordinates.

underlying_signals.label: Shared text/body semantics
underlying_signals.reason: Viewport drift plus newline/Creole source markers usually means multiline Display splitting, block height, or body padding accumulation is off upstream of final SVG.

fix_suggestions.label: Shared richtext table/display
fix_suggestions.reason: Rust and Java disagree on Creole/table structure, so start in the shared Display/Creole parser-renderer path instead of the Graphviz handoff.

fix_suggestions.label: Shared text/body height
fix_suggestions.reason: The underlying signals point to upstream Display or Creole semantics, so investigate text splitting and block measurement before touching downstream coordinates.
